stem: A digital marketing study surveys 20 users shown the ad and observes 4 positive outcomes out of 20 (20.0%). The sample contains two subgroups: Group A (6 observations, rate = 16.7%) and Group B (14 observations, rate = 21.4%). The true population composition is uncertain: Group A makes up between 35% and 45% of the population. Estimate the true click-through rate and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Simple pooled estimate: 0.2. Weighted estimate (stratified): 0.1842 (range [0.1804, 0.1879]). Combined CI accounting for stratification uncertainty: [0.0807, 0.416]. Point estimate: 0.1842.

solution_steps: Stratified estimate: weighted by population composition. At w_A=0.4: 0.4×0.139 + 0.6×0.2143 = 0.1842.
common_failure_modes: Using the Wald interval (p̂ ± z√(p̂(1-p̂)/n)) which performs poorly for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
Not recognizing that with n=20, the normal approximation may be poor
Ignoring the population composition uncertainty and using the simple pooled rate
